currentUse: Bellesini Academy — a tuition-free, independent Catholic middle school (grades 5-8, about 120 students) operating at 94 Bradford St, Lawrence, MA 01840; actively running as of its 2026 graduation ceremonies.
summary: The 1921 stone church of St. Francis (Šv. Pranciškaus) Lithuanian parish at 94 Bradford Street, Lawrence, is standing and in active use: since the parish's last service in 2002 it has housed Bellesini Academy, a tuition-free independent Catholic middle school founded that same year, which currently enrolls about 120 students in grades 5-8 and held graduation ceremonies in 2026. Canonically, St. Francis was merged with Lawrence's other national parishes — Holy Rosary (Italian), Holy Trinity (Polish), and Saints Peter & Paul (Portuguese) — into the territorial Corpus Christi Parish, formally established by the Archdiocese of Boston on November 1, 2004; on December 1, 2021 that parish became Holy Rosary Shrine, administered by the Franciscans of the Poor Christ. The parish's roughly 13-acre lakeside summer estate at Forest Lake in Methuen was sold for residential development after the parish was disestablished; a district of Lithuanian-named streets (Palanga, Varniai, Kaunas, Luoke, Birute, Vytis) remains there. Traces of the Lithuanian heritage survive: one stained-glass window was moved to the Central Catholic High School chapel with an explanatory plaque, and a replica of the Our Lady of Vilnius painting — one that toured Lithuanian DP camps after 1944 — hangs at the successor parish at 35 Essex Street, where the community celebrated Lithuania's independence centennial with a Lithuanian-language Mass in February 2018. The school building shows no outward signs of its Lithuanian past.

developments.headline: Last service held; altar removed as parish closes
developments.detail: Bishop Emilio Allue of the Greater Merrimack region watched attendants remove the altar of St. Francis Lithuanian Church at the end of the last service there in 2002 (Eagle-Tribune file photo caption). St. Francis and other Lawrence churches were merged into Corpus Christi Parish.

developments.headline: Bellesini Academy founded and occupies the Bradford Street church building
developments.detail: Bellesini Academy, a tuition-free independent Catholic middle school for Lawrence students of limited financial means, was founded in 2002 and operates at 94 Bradford St. The Eagle-Tribune (2018) confirmed the Bradford Street building 'now houses Bellesini Academy, a Catholic middle school.' The school remains active, with about 120 students and 2026 graduation ceremonies noted on its website.

sources.title: St. Francis Roman Catholic Church (Lawrence, MA)

developments.headline: Corpus Christi Parish formally established, absorbing St. Francis
developments.detail: The Archdiocese of Boston consolidated four historic national parishes — St. Francis (Lithuanian, 1903), Holy Rosary (Italian, 1904), Holy Trinity (Polish, 1905), and Saints Peter & Paul (Portuguese, 1907) — into the territorial Corpus Christi Parish at Holy Rosary Church.

developments.headline: Forest Lake summer estate in Methuen sold for residential development
developments.detail: St. Francis parish owned approximately 13 acres around Forest Lake in Methuen as a recreational retreat for parishioners. After the parish was disestablished, the property was sold for residential construction. A district of Lithuanian-named streets (Palanga, Varniai, Kaunas, Luoke, Birute, Vytis) occupies the former resort area. No sale price, buyer, or year was published in sources opened.

developments.headline: Lithuanian independence centennial celebrated at successor parish
developments.detail: The Lithuanian American Council and Knights of Lithuania Council 78 organized a celebration of Lithuania's independence centennial at Corpus Christi Parish (Union and Essex streets), with a Mass in Lithuanian and English celebrated by Rev. Aurelijus Gricius, OFM, of the Lithuanian Franciscan monastery in Kennebunk, Maine, followed by a dinner with Lithuanian food and the Boston dance group Sambūris. Organizer Jonas Stundza noted about 5,000 Lithuanian immigrants had settled in Lawrence in the mill era.

developments.headline: Successor parish becomes Holy Rosary Shrine
developments.detail: Corpus Christi Parish — the canonical successor holding St. Francis's legacy — became Holy Rosary Shrine, administered by the Franciscans of the Poor Christ. A replica of the Our Lady of Vilnius painting, which had toured Lithuanian refugee camps after 1944, hangs at the 35 Essex Street site, where some Lithuanian activities have continued.

sources.title: St. Francis Roman Catholic Church (Lawrence, MA)

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions. Refinements: (1) Sources confirm the last service and closure in 2002, but the exact July 1, 2002 date in the archive record could not be independently verified in sources opened. (2) The archive's "lakeside summer estate" matches the ~13-acre Forest Lake retreat in Methuen; Global True Lithuania confirms it was sold for residential development after the parish was disestablished, consistent with the archive's note that the archdiocese appropriated the property. (3) The canonical merger was formalized on November 1, 2004 as Corpus Christi Parish (four national parishes united) — the 2002 date reflects the closing of the St. Francis church itself, a distinction worth capturing. (4) The church building was not sold to secular use: it houses Bellesini Academy, an independent Catholic school founded in 2002, so the physical fate is conversion within Catholic-affiliated use rather than demolition or secular sale.
gaps: Not found despite searching: any published sale price, buyer name, or deed year for either the church building or the Forest Lake estate. A search-result snippet attributed to a 2004 Boston Globe article claimed the City of Methuen bought 13 acres of the former St. Francis property, keeping 11 acres as conservation land and rezoning 6 house lots (one containing a cinder-block "St. Francis Hall"), but the underlying article could not be opened and this remains unverified — it also partly conflicts with True Lithuania's simpler "sold for residential development" account. Unknown whether Bellesini Academy owns the building or leases it from the archdiocese. Current (post-2021) frequency of Lithuanian-language Mass or Lithuanian community activity at Holy Rosary Shrine could not be confirmed from the shrine's own site; a mass-times aggregator snippet listed a Lithuanian Mass as "T.B.D." but was not opened. The exact July 1, 2002 closure date was not independently confirmed. No preservation campaign, demolition threat, or recent (2020s) press specifically about the Bradford Street building was found beyond the school's ongoing operation.
